subject: Sociology
title: Surveillance and Social Control
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Surveillance and Social Control.

hook.text: CCTV cameras watch you walk. Your phone tracks you. Your browser history is stored. Who's watching?

buildup.text: Foucault described the panopticon: a prison where inmates behave because they might be watched at any moment.

discovery.text: Modern surveillance extends this logic to entire populations. Self-discipline replaces direct physical control.

twist.text: Surveillance doesn't fall equally. Minority communities, activists, and the poor are watched far more closely.

climax.text: Technology made surveillance invisible and total. Social control now operates through data, not just force.

punchline.text: You behave because you might be watched.

quiz.question: What is Foucault's panopticon concept?
options: People self-regulate because they might be observed
